title: Championship January transfers: Confirmed deals and net spend for 2026 winter window
text: The Championship's January 2026 transfer window proved to be an active period, with clubs making strategic moves to strengthen their squads ahead of the promotion push. Overall spending across all Championship clubs totaled £37 million, with income of £10.1 million, resulting in a net spend of £26.9 million. Several marquee signings highlighted the window. Recently-relegated Ipswich Town secured long-serving Sunderland midfielder Dan Neil on loan until season's end, described as a two-time promotion winner who was a fundamental part of the Black Cats' rise from the League One doldrums to the Premier League. Norwich City invested £4.34 million in permanent additions, signing Ali Ahmed from Vancouver Whitecaps and Mohamed Toure from Randers. They also captured Manchester United left-back Harry Amass on loan. Southampton emerged as a major seller, generating £15.8 million in income, including the £7 million sale of striker Adam Armstrong to Wolverhampton Wanderers and the £4.5 million departure of defender Armel Bella-Kotchap to Hellas Verona. Queens Park Rangers and Blackburn Rovers each invested £4.5 million and £3.5 million respectively, targeting defensive reinforcements. Birmingham City, Derby County, Portsmouth, and numerous other clubs operated primarily through loan deals, which remained incredibly popular given their financial advantages for clubs outside the Premier League. The window featured extensive loan activity, with Manchester City, Liverpool, Brighton, Chelsea, and other top-flight clubs using loan arrangements to develop talent while supporting Championship sides' promotion aspirations.
